title: A Relational Model of Data for Large Shared Data Banks
author: E. F. Codd
note: The original 1970 argument for data independence and normalized relations — short, readable, and striking for how many 'modern' data-model debates Codd anticipated.

title: What Goes Around Comes Around... And Around...
author: Michael Stonebraker & Andrew Pavlo
note: Surveys two decades of post-relational contenders — MapReduce, document, graph, array, vector — and explains why each was largely absorbed back into SQL and relational systems.

title: CMU 15-445/645 Intro to Database Systems (Fall 2024) — Lecture Schedule
author: Andy Pavlo / Carnegie Mellon University
note: Free full lecture videos and slides; Lectures 1-2 work through relational algebra and modern SQL (window functions, recursive CTEs) with worked examples the chapter only sketches.

title: Turning the Database Inside Out with Apache Samza (Strange Loop 2014)
author: Martin Kleppmann
note: The book's author motivates event sourcing and CQRS from first principles by deconstructing a database into its replication log, indexes, and materialized views.

title: Event Sourcing
author: Martin Fowler
note: The canonical pattern write-up, with concrete guidance on event replay, temporal queries, reversing events, and the tricky interplay with external systems.

title: Neo4j Cypher Manual — Introduction
author: Neo4j
note: Authoritative reference for the ASCII-art pattern syntax, MATCH/WHERE semantics, and variable-length path queries behind the chapter's property-graph examples.

title: Learn GraphQL — Official Documentation
author: GraphQL Foundation
note: Runnable examples of queries, fragments, variables, and the schema type system that clarify why GraphQL is a client-facing API language rather than a database query language.

title: Towards Scalable Dataframe Systems
author: Devin Petersohn et al. (VLDB 2020)
note: Formalizes dataframe semantics — ordering, flexible schemas, row/column symmetry — and explains exactly why pandas-style APIs resist classic relational optimization.
